title: Sitemap XML, Index Traversal, Location Rules, and Admission · TypeScript Build Room
summary: A disability-led public-interest technology team incrementally builds bounded sitemap discovery ledger from original deterministic HTTP, robots, sitemap, HTML, browser, and report fixtures while retaining prior TypeScript, Node, web, Git, accessibility, security, testing, and recovery requirements. Planned artifact: Build a complete first version of bounded sitemap discovery ledger while every earlier competency remains active. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case…
objectives: Validate sitemap and sitemap-index namespaces, UTF-8 XML, escaping, location count, size, and required loc values.
Bound index depth, files, URLs, bytes, redirects, duplicate entries, duration, and cycles before traversal.
Resolve and admit sitemap and page locations through authorization, origin, robots, URL, and SSRF policy.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Authorized site auditing and TypeScript Commit to the mission model · Workshop Sitemap Format
steps.instruction:
workshop case — A workshop TypeScript team handles case 8320 during an authorized municipal service audit. Build deterministic TypeScript evidence to admit bounded sitemap XML and index graphs through host, scope, hint, and provenance policy; URL, robots, body, parser, frontier, privacy, accessibility, and report budgets stay explicit; then change one owner permission, seed, URL, robots, sitemap, redirect, DNS, media, or encoding decision and reconcile provenance. Browser TypeScript uses pure bounded models and original URL, robots, sitemap, HTTP, HTML, frontier, accessibility, browser, and report fixtures only. Real DNS, Fetch, streams, Cheerio, Playwright, axe-core, files, processes, packages, containers, assistive-technology review, load, legal review, restore, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates.

What must be established before implementation?

Evidence target: Authorized site auditing and TypeScript Commit to the mission model · Workshop Sitemap Format.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for validate sitemap and sitemap-index namespaces, utf-8 xml, escaping, location count, size, and required loc values. For Authorized site auditing and TypeScript Commit to the mission model · Workshop Sitemap Format, keep Sitemap Format connected to executable TypeScript authorization, URL, runtime admission, Fetch, Promise, browser, accessibility, report, package, and recovery decision evidence.
